hero.name: Hoang Anh
hero.description: I am about to complete my Bachelor's degree in Computer Science at IUT de Créteil-Vitry (Université Paris-Est Créteil). Motivated and curious, I design technical projects and aim to progress in the field of AI.

aife.description: Development and industrialization of an Angular/TypeScript component library for the French government with RGAA compliance. Implementation of a Figma plugin for token export and management. Fullstack contribution to the ASTRA project and POCs to validate component adaptability.

kaist.description: Design of a graphical interface with PyQt5 for real-time visualization of physiological data (pMMG, EMG, IMU). Development of a deep learning pipeline with TensorFlow/Keras (RNN, GRU, LSTM, Transformer).

medical.description: No-code medical web platform with React (TypeScript) and Java (Gradle) for dynamic medical workflows. Firebase security and deployment with Node.js.

game-of-life.title: Game of Life - Multithreaded Simulation
game-of-life.description: Interactive simulation of Conway's Game of Life with a modern graphical interface and parallelized computations via multi-threading.
game-of-life.longDescription: Cellular automaton conceived by John Conway in 1970. Each cell has its own thread for parallel computations, synchronized via a barrier (threading.Barrier). The grid is configurable from 5×5 to 80×80 cells (up to 6,400 simultaneous threads), with a 30 FPS refresh rate and a simulation speed of 1 to 30 generations/second.
features: High-performance multi-threading: one thread per cell, up to 6,400 simultaneous threads
Barrier synchronization: no thread starts generation N+1 before all have finished N
Full history of the last 100 generations (Undo/Redo)
4 predefined themes (Dark, Neon, Ocean, Sunset) + custom theme creator
Integrated interactive tutorial: 12 pages with classic patterns (Block, Blinker, Glider)
Automatic session saving on close with optional resume on restart
Real-time controls: Play/Pause, step-by-step (+1 Gen), variable speed (1–30 gen/s)
Direct interaction: click to toggle a cell, click + drag to draw continuously
techDetails: Double buffering: T (n+2)×(n+2) and Tnext grids to avoid read/write conflicts
Control events: running, stop_event, step_event, redraw_event
Modular architecture: 6 separate files (gamelife_core, gui_game, gui_windows, gui_components, history_manager, theme_manager)
4 auto-generated JSON files: gamelife_config, custom_themes, favorite_colors, gamelife_history
Patterns: Observer (UI events), Singleton (global configuration)
Optional Pillow (PIL) for improved color picker performance

exoskeleton-deep-learning-interface.title: Deep Learning Interface for Exoskeleton
exoskeleton-deep-learning-interface.org: KAIST · Korea Advanced Institute of Science & Technology
exoskeleton-deep-learning-interface.description: Design of a fullstack graphical interface for designing, training, and evaluating Deep Learning models applied to human motion analysis for exoskeleton control.
exoskeleton-deep-learning-interface.longDescription: As part of my internship at the Exo Lab at KAIST (South Korea), I developed a Python application to create and train neural networks for the analysis of biomechanical data (EMG, IMU) used in exoskeleton control. The tool offers a complete pipeline: dataset generation, model configuration (LSTM, GRU, RNN, Transformer), training with real-time monitoring, performance evaluation and model comparison. Project carried out in collaboration with Thanh Uyen NGUYEN.
features: Dynamic model building: LSTM, GRU, RNN, Transformer and TinyTransformer
Asynchronous multithreaded training with reactive interface and real-time monitoring
Comprehensive evaluation: confusion matrices, prediction vs ground truth curves, scatter plots
Built-in optimizations: EarlyStopping, ReduceLROnPlateau, class_weight
Support for multi-class classification and regression tasks
Save and load models in HDF5 format
Side-by-side model comparison
EMG/IMU signal preprocessing, sliding windows, normalization
techDetails: Fullstack Python architecture: TensorFlow/Keras backend, PyQt5 frontend
Asynchronous management with QThread and custom callbacks
Dynamic model building with build_flexible_model
Data pipeline: HDF5 file loading, EMG signal filtering, automatic label generation
Design patterns: MVC, centralized state management, observers (PyQt signals)
Strict modularity: training_core/, windows/, utils/
Bidirectionality of RNN layers, attention mechanisms (Transformer), temporal windowing

olga-workflow-platform.title: OLGA · No-code Workflow Platform
olga-workflow-platform.description: No-code workflow management platform orchestrated by a Spring Boot/Java engine, with three React frontends (Designer, Admin, Business) and a hybrid MySQL/Firebase architecture. Dynamic form generation with Zod validation.
olga-workflow-platform.longDescription: As part of an end-of-degree academic project in the BUT Computer Science program, I contributed to the development of OLGA, a no-code workflow management platform. Architecture: Spring Boot/Java engine, React/TypeScript frontends (Designer, Admin, Business), hybrid MySQL/Firebase persistence, dynamic forms with Zod validation. Docker containerization, one-command deployment. Project carried out in collaboration with LISSI and tested on medical, logistics, and field management scenarios.
features: No-code workflow design via visual interface (Designer)
Dynamic form generation with Zod validation
Multi-step process orchestration with conditional transitions
Hybrid MySQL/Firebase architecture: structured persistence + real-time
Docker containerization with Docker Compose (5 services)
Three React frontends: Designer (8080), Admin (8081), Business
Push notifications and real-time synchronization via Firebase
One-command deployment: docker compose up -d --build
techDetails: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Zod, Axios, Firebase SDK
Backend: Java 17, Spring Boot, REST API, JPA/Hibernate
Database: MySQL 8 (persistence), Firebase Firestore (real-time)
Containerization: Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx for SPAs
Services: Designer (8080), Admin (8081), API (9091), MySQL (3306), phpMyAdmin (81)
Reusable React form interpreter (FormInterpreter)
Infrastructure deployable on any Linux server
